description: Asteroid Belt Development → Belter Civilization. A per-world timeline unlocked when the player colonizes Ceres. Tracks the emergence of a distinct spacefaring culture from mining outpost to Dyson swarm supplier. The Expanse meets Soviet central planning: Belters as a new Soviet nationality, born in microgravity, raised on quotas, dreaming of autonomy. Grounded in hard SF (Corey's Expanse, KSR's 2312, Niven's Belters) and orbital mechanics. No handwaving. Delta-v is king.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: SECOND ASTEROID SECURED FOR SOCIALIST MINING: Quota Immediately Doubled
narrative.toast: A second rock has been claimed, surveyed, and assigned a production target. The quota is exactly double the first asteroid's output. The fact that the second asteroid is compositionally different and requires entirely new extraction equipment has not been factored into the plan.
narrative.description: The first asteroid was a proof of concept. The second is a quota obligation. Moscow reasons simply: two rocks, twice the minerals. That the second asteroid is a carbonaceous chondrite (water and organics) while the first was metallic (iron-nickel) is a detail the planning commission has classified as 'operationally irrelevant.' The mining crews, now splitting between two sites 40,000 km apart, have been informed that shift lengths remain unchanged. Transit time is not counted as labor.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: REGULAR ORE TRANSPORT OPERATIONAL: Delta-V Optimized Trajectories Serve the Plan
narrative.toast: Hohmann transfer orbits between belt stations and the inner system have been computed and scheduled. Transit time: 2-4 years depending on orbital alignment. The cargo manifest must be filed 3 years in advance. The Ministry of Interplanetary Logistics considers this an improvement over terrestrial rail scheduling.
narrative.description: Shipping in the asteroid belt is not like shipping on Earth. There are no roads, only orbital mechanics. Each transfer window opens for weeks and closes for years. A missed window means cargo sits in vacuum for 26 months waiting for the next alignment. The shipping schedule is a 4-dimensional optimization problem involving the orbital positions of every asteroid, every station, and every planet. Gosplan has assigned it to a department of three clerks with an abacus. They are behind schedule but claim the abacus is performing within acceptable parameters.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: MICROGRAVITY MANUFACTURING EXCEEDS ALL QUOTAS: Perfect Spheres for the Motherland
narrative.toast: In zero gravity, molten metal forms perfect spheres. Crystal growth proceeds without convection defects. Pharmaceutical proteins fold correctly on the first attempt. The ball bearings alone are worth more than the entire station. The workers who produce them have not received a raise.
narrative.description: Microgravity manufacturing produces items physically impossible to create on Earth: perfect ball bearings (no gravitational deformation), flawless optical fibers (no convection-induced impurities), pharmaceutical crystals of unprecedented purity (no sedimentation). Each product category has been assigned its own Five-Year Plan. The combined weight of the paperwork exceeds the mass of the products. The factory workers, who float rather than stand at their stations, have petitioned for a new trudodni category: 'weightless labor.' The petition was denied. Labor is labor regardless of gravitational context, the Ministry replied.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: NEW SOVIET NATIONALITY RECOGNIZED: 'Belters' Granted Cultural Autonomy Within Framework of Socialist Unity
narrative.toast: The second generation born in microgravity is taller, thinner, with elongated limbs and enlarged hearts. They cannot walk on Earth. They speak Russian with a clipped, air-conserving dialect. They call themselves 'kamenshchiki' — the rock people. Moscow has added a new line to the census form.
narrative.description: Corey's Expanse depicted Belters as a distinct human culture shaped by microgravity: taller, thinner, with brittle bones that shatter in planetary gravity. The reality is emerging. Children born and raised in 0.001-0.03g develop differently: elongated spines, redistribution of body fluids, cardiac adaptation, reduced bone density. By the second generation, they are physically incapable of visiting Earth. A new Soviet nationality — the 135th — has been proclaimed. They have been assigned a folk dance. Performing it in microgravity requires a specially modified cultural center with handrails. The cultural commissar responsible for approving the dance has never visited the belt.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: HOLLOWED ASTEROID DECLARED HABITABLE: Self-Contained Biosphere Serves 500 Workers
narrative.toast: A 2-km asteroid has been hollowed out, spun for 0.3g artificial gravity, fitted with fusion-powered grow lights, and filled with atmosphere. Inside: forests, a stream, 47 species of plants, and 500 workers who have not seen actual sunlight in six years. They report that the artificial sunset is 'adequate.'
narrative.description: KSR's '2312' describes 'terraria' — hollowed asteroids turned into rotating habitats with self-contained biospheres. The engineering: mine the asteroid hollow, spin it for centripetal gravity along the interior surface, seal it, fill it with atmosphere, plant an ecosystem. The result is a cylinder world — O'Neill's vision built inside a rock. The Soviet version includes mandatory collective farming plots, a portrait of Lenin at the administrative center (which is technically on the ceiling from the perspective of the entrance), and a public address system that echoes badly in the curved interior. The first tomato grown inside the terrarium was celebrated with a ceremony. The second was requisitioned for the quota.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: INTER-ASTEROID COMMERCIAL NETWORK FORMALIZED: Barter System Operates Within Planned Economy Framework
narrative.toast: Twelve asteroid stations now trade directly with each other. Station A has excess water ice. Station B has surplus iron. Station C manufactures ball bearings. They trade amongst themselves and file the paperwork afterward. Moscow has declared this 'planned barter' rather than 'market activity.' The distinction is ideologically critical and economically meaningless.
narrative.description: The belt economy develops organically despite central planning, not because of it. Signal delay to Earth (15-25 minutes) means real-time coordination with Moscow is impossible. Local station chiefs make trading decisions and report them as 'pre-approved plan fulfillment' after the fact. A hybrid economy emerges: official quotas coexist with unofficial barter. Water ice trades for refined metals. Refined metals trade for manufactured goods. Manufactured goods trade for food. The entire system operates on trust, reputation, and the shared understanding that if Moscow discovers the extent of unauthorized trading, everyone involved will be reassigned to surface mining. This is the belt's version of the Soviet shadow economy — the tolkachi (fixers) now operate across interplanetary distances.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: 16 PSYCHE MINING OPERATIONS COMMENCE: Iron-Nickel Asteroid Contains More Metal Than Entire Earth Crust
narrative.toast: 16 Psyche: 226 km diameter, pure iron-nickel core of a destroyed protoplanet. Estimated value: $10,000 quadrillion. The Ministry of Finance has attempted to add this to the state balance sheet. The resulting number crashed the accounting system. A new accounting system has been requisitioned.
narrative.description: 16 Psyche is the exposed iron-nickel core of a protoplanet destroyed in the early solar system. It contains approximately 1.7x10^19 kg of iron-nickel alloy — enough to supply Earth's current iron consumption for several million years. The economic implications are staggering: metal scarcity, which has driven human conflict for 10,000 years, effectively ends. The planning commission has responded by increasing the metal production quota by 10,000%. The quota for processing the quota increase paperwork has also been increased. Workers on Psyche's surface report that the entire asteroid is magnetic, their tools stick to the ground, and compass-based navigation is 'philosophically challenging.' The surface gravity is 0.006g — a worker who trips will orbit the asteroid before landing.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: BELT ADMINISTRATIVE RESTRUCTURING PROPOSAL: Local Governance Adjustments Under Review (CLASSIFIED)
narrative.toast: Signal delay to Earth: 15-25 minutes. By the time Moscow's response arrives, the situation has already changed. Belt station chiefs have been making autonomous decisions for a generation. Now they want to make it official. The petition for 'enhanced local governance' is 340 pages. Moscow has assigned a committee to read it. The committee meets quarterly.
narrative.description: The Expanse's central tension manifests: Belters, physically unable to return to Earth, culturally distinct, economically self-sufficient, and separated by 15-25 minute signal delay from central authority, begin agitating for autonomy. They do not use the word 'independence' — that would trigger Article 7 of the Space Territories Governance Act. Instead, they request 'enhanced administrative flexibility within the framework of socialist self-determination.' The petition cites Lenin's own writings on the rights of nationalities. Moscow is furious but cannot easily refute Lenin with Lenin. The KGB's Belt Division (three agents, one of whom has gone native) reports that the movement is 'contained.' The report takes 25 minutes to reach Earth. By the time it arrives, two more stations have signed the petition.

narrative.pravdaHeadline: BELT POPULATION MILESTONE: 10,000 PERMANENT RESIDENTS ACROSS 23 HABITATS
narrative.toast: Ten thousand souls live permanently among the rocks. They are born in terraria, educated in zero-g classrooms, apprenticed in mining operations, and buried (or more precisely, recycled — mass is precious) in the stations where they lived. The census form now includes 'gravitational preference' as a demographic category. Most Belters list '0.01g or below.'
narrative.description: The belt has crossed from outpost to civilization. 10,000 permanent residents across 23 habitats — 7 terraria, 12 mining stations, 4 manufacturing complexes. Birth rate exceeds death rate for the third consecutive year. A school system has been established (curriculum: standard Soviet plus orbital mechanics, vacuum safety, and 'Introduction to Not Dying in Space'). A hospital treats conditions unknown on Earth: vacuum exposure decompression, micrometeorite wounds, and 'rock fever' — a psychological condition in which the patient becomes convinced their asteroid is shrinking. The Ministry of Health has classified rock fever as 'occupational' rather than 'psychiatric' to avoid pension implications. The belt's first poet has published a collection titled 'Songs from the Void.' It sold 47 copies. The cultural commissar pronounced it 'ideologically sound but gravitationally confused.'

narrative.pravdaHeadline: BELT MINING INFRASTRUCTURE REPURPOSED: ASTEROID MATERIALS SERVE DYSON SWARM CONSTRUCTION — THE ULTIMATE QUOTA
narrative.toast: Every asteroid in the belt is now a potential solar collector component. The mining operation that began with one rock and three workers has become the supply chain for the largest engineering project in human history. The quota: disassemble the asteroid belt. Delivery schedule: 10,000 years. The Belters, who have spent generations building a civilization among these rocks, have been informed that their home is now raw material. They are taking it about as well as expected.
narrative.description: The Dyson swarm requires mass — approximately 2x10^26 kg of material to construct a shell of solar collectors around the Sun. The asteroid belt contains roughly 3x10^21 kg — a fraction of the total, but the easiest to access. Belt mining infrastructure, built over generations for ore extraction, is repurposed for wholesale asteroid disassembly. This is the Soviet system's ultimate expression: the entire asteroid belt as a resource to be consumed in service of the Plan. The Belters face an existential crisis: their civilization exists ON the resource being extracted. Mining your own home is a metaphor Soviet citizens have understood since collectivization. The planning commission has assured belt residents that 'sufficient habitable mass will be retained.' The definition of 'sufficient' has not been shared. The Five-Year Plan for Dyson Swarm Material Supply runs to 47,000 pages. It is the longest document in human history. Nobody has read it in its entirety, including the authors.
